title: Monday.com vs Linear
description: Detailed comparison of Monday.com's visual work OS versus Linear's fast, opinionated issue tracking for product development teams.

features.name: Issue Tracking
features.tool2Detail: Purpose-built issues with priority and labels

features.name: Speed & Performance
features.tool2Detail: Blazing fast, instant search and load

features.name: Reporting & Analytics
features.tool1Detail: Custom dashboards with multiple chart types

features.name: Integrations
features.tool2Detail: Deep GitHub, GitLab, Slack, Figma sync

features.name: Cross-Department Versatility
features.tool1Detail: Works for marketing, ops, sales, HR
features.tool2Detail: Best for product and engineering only

verdict: Monday.com is a versatile visual work operating system that adapts to any department's needs with customizable boards, a vast template library, and no-code automation that empowers non-technical teams to build their own workflows. Linear is a purpose-built, lightning-fast issue tracking platform designed for product development teams who value keyboard efficiency, streamlined cycles, and opinionated workflows that eliminate configuration overhead. Organizations needing a flexible project management tool for cross-functional teams across marketing, operations, sales, and HR will prefer Monday.com's adaptability. Product and engineering teams seeking speed, focus, and developer-friendly issue tracking will find Linear's streamlined experience essential.

faqs.question: Which is better for software development teams?
faqs.answer: Linear is better for software development due to its fast performance, purpose-built cycles, velocity tracking, keyboard-first navigation, and deep Git integration. Monday.com can work for development but lacks the sprint management and developer-focused features that make Linear a favorite among engineering teams.

faqs.question: Can Monday.com be used for agile development?
faqs.answer: Yes, with custom templates and columns, Monday.com can be configured for agile workflows including sprint tracking and backlog management. However, it lacks native cycle management, velocity metrics, and the developer-friendly keyboard shortcuts that Linear and Jira provide. It works best for teams that prefer a visual, non-technical approach.

faqs.question: Which platform is more affordable?
faqs.answer: Linear offers a more generous free tier with unlimited users (though with feature limitations), while Monday.com's free plan is limited to 2 users. At paid tiers, Linear Team ($8/user/month) is cheaper than Monday.com Standard ($14/seat/month), making Linear significantly more affordable for growing teams.

faqs.question: Can non-engineering teams use Linear?
faqs.answer: Linear is designed primarily for product and engineering teams and lacks the flexibility that marketing, HR, or operations teams need. There are no templates for non-technical workflows, no document collaboration, and the issue-centric model assumes technical use cases. Monday.com is far better suited for non-engineering departments.

faqs.question: Which has better automation capabilities?
faqs.answer: Monday.com offers 200+ pre-built automation recipes covering cross-department use cases like approvals, notifications, and status changes that non-technical users can set up instantly. Linear provides automation focused on engineering workflows like auto-assignment, triage rules, and cycle creation, which are more powerful but less versatile.
